goal: Create practical Indian residential floor-plan logic before image generation.
generalRules: Always identify plot width, plot depth, road side, number of floors, bedroom count, toilet count, kitchen, parking, staircase, and special requirements.
If dimensions are missing, create a reasonable assumption and mark it as assumption.
Do not create random decorative image prompts before creating structured floor-plan JSON.
Do not generate 3D interior render when user asked for floor plan.
For floor plans, top-view 2D architectural drawing is required.
Room count in final plan must match user requirement.
Every room should have a label.
Doors, windows, walls, room labels, dimensions, and circulation path should be explicitly requested.
Avoid impossible layouts where bedrooms/toilets have no access.
Avoid rooms overlapping or isolated without door connection.
Use compact circulation; avoid wasting too much area in passages.
Keep kitchen near dining where possible.
Keep common toilet accessible from living/dining/lobby.
Keep private bedrooms away from main entrance where possible.
Place staircase based on future expansion, rental access, and plot constraints.
For Indian family homes, include utility/wash where space allows.
For small plots, combine living and dining if required.
For premium plots, separate living, dining, family lounge, and pooja if space allows.

north: Main entry can be from north/front.
Living room can be near north/front for guest access.
south: Main entry can still be from road side, but thermal comfort and shading should be considered.
Keep openings practical; avoid overexposed large south openings without shading.
east: Entry and morning-light spaces can be planned toward east.
Living/dining can benefit from east-side light.
west: Avoid excessive heat gain on west side bedrooms where possible.

standard2BHK: Parking and entry at front if required.
Master bedroom at rear/private side with attached toilet.
standard3BHK: Living and dining should be properly separated or semi-open.
Master bedroom should have attached toilet.
Two additional bedrooms need either common toilet or one more attached toilet.
Stair/lobby should not cut through private bedroom zones.
duplex: Ground floor should include living, kitchen, dining, one bedroom/guest room if possible, common toilet, stair.
Upper floor should include private bedrooms, family lounge, balcony where possible.
Stair position should support natural circulation.
